verses.1: These are the words of Agur son of Jakeh—the burden that this man declared to Ithiel:
verses.15: There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, ‘Enough!’:
verses.16: Sheol, the barren womb, land never satisfied with water, and fire that never says, ‘Enough!’
verses.18: There are three things too wonderful for me, four that I cannot understand:
verses.19: the way of an eagle in the sky, the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship at sea, and the way of a man with a maiden.
verses.21: Under three things the earth trembles, under four it cannot bear up:
verses.22: a servant who becomes king, a fool who is filled with food,
verses.23: an unloved woman who marries, and a maidservant who supplants her mistress.
verses.24: Four things on earth are small, yet they are exceedingly wise:
verses.25: The ants are creatures of little strength, yet they store up their food in the summer;
verses.26: the rock badgers are creatures of little power, yet they make their homes in the rocks;
verses.27: the locusts have no king, yet they all advance in formation;
verses.28: and the lizard can be caught in one’s hands, yet it is found in the palaces of kings.
verses.29: There are three things that are stately in their stride, and four that are impressive in their walk:
verses.30: a lion, mighty among beasts, refusing to retreat before anything;
verses.31: a strutting rooster; a he-goat; and a king with his army around him.
